title: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline)
public_claim: A defensive-RAG governance doctrine whose first job is to stop immature security claims from inflating themselves.
distinctive_mechanism: Every claim must carry mission profile, attacker tier, objective class, evidence packet, current boundary, residual risk, and downgrade triggers before it can mature through C1 Designed, C2 Implemented, C3 Validated, C4 Adaptively Stress-Tested, C5 Operationally Sustainable, and C6 Externally Defensible stages, with NIST AI RMF, NIST SP, and Google SAIF-style controls as reference discipline.
use_value: Its value is the language firewall: it makes words like secure, robust, solved, government-grade, and production-ready unavailable until evidence earns them.

component_proofs.component: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) Proof Boundary
component_proofs.claim_supported: Every control in the lab currently sits at the first lifecycle gate — Designed. Nothing has been promoted to Implemented, because nothing is yet implemented; the lab has chosen to wait rather than promote on documentation alone. This is the discipline working.
component_proofs.input: built assets, partial assets, archive-only assets, and unsupported claims

component_proofs.component: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) Promotion Route
component_proofs.claim_supported: The lab is not waiting to write more doctrine. It is waiting to acquire a real defensive operation to govern, and then to graduate the controls one at a time by producing the kind of evidence each gate demands. The order is fixed by what the lab is.
component_proofs.input: website room, MuranoOS room, Nexus task, paper, source bank, or archive
component_proofs.expected_output: where the packet should go next as a reviewable artifact

claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
